text: Hey, can I get access to the Nexus repo for the prod-payments service? Need to push a hotfix ASAP.

I just got a weird email from "Veridian Support" asking me to reset my Nexus credentials—it looks off, the logo's wrong and the link goes to a sketchy domain. Should I forward it somewhere?
My laptop was stolen from my car last night and it had full disk encryption but I'm still freaking out—what's the next step for reporting this?
I logged into Helix from my home IP yesterday and just got an alert about a login from Singapore 20 minutes ago. I'm not traveling—someone else is in my account.
Someone just called me claiming to be from IT security and asked for my Prism password—red flags everywhere, I hung up but wanted to flag it.
I clicked a link in an email that looked like it was from our vendor and now my screen is locked with a ransom note demanding 0.5 BTC. What do I do?
Noticed a weird process called "xmrig" running on my workstation—CPU is maxed out and I didn't install anything mining-related.
My badge was used to enter the data center at 3 AM last night and I was definitely asleep in bed—how do I check who's messing with my access?
I forwarded a suspicious email to phishing@veridian but just realized it had a PDF attachment that might've auto-downloaded—should I disconnect from the network?
Got a Slack DM from someone I don't know asking for help with “urgent prod-payments access”—they're not in my org chart and the tone is pushy.
I left my phone in a rideshare last night and it had the Authenticator app on it—how do I revoke MFA before someone uses it?
Noticed an unfamiliar GitHub token in my Nexus settings with admin permissions—no idea how it got there or who created it.

Someone tried to reset my Okta password three times in the last hour and I didn't initiate any of them.
I think I fell for a phishing test but the email looked *exactly* like the real IT security bulletin—where do I report this?

I found a USB drive in the parking lot labeled “Q4 Bonuses” and plugged it in—now my desktop has a weird EXE running in the background.
Noticed an unauthorized SSH key added to my prod-payments server—it's not mine and I'm the only one with access.

Someone just tried to tailgate me into the office with a fake badge—security saw it but I wanted to log it here too.
I accidentally sent my API key to a Slack channel instead of DM'ing it—how do I rotate it ASAP?
My laptop started running super slow and Task Manager shows a process called “ransomware.exe”—I killed it but I'm worried.
I got an email from “IT Support” with a link to “update your Helix credentials” but the domain is veridian-security[.]net.
Noticed a new admin user in our Nexus repo that no one on the team recognizes—could this be a backdoor?

My screen went black for a second and then showed a message saying “Pay 0.1 BTC to unlock your files”—what's the protocol?

Found a weird cron job on my machine that's sending data to an external IP every 5 minutes—I didn't set it up.

Got an alert that my VPN session was active in two locations at once—one in SF and one in Ukraine.
I forwarded a suspicious email to the security team but just realized it might've had a malicious macro—should I wipe my machine?
Noticed an unauthorized Slack integration in my workspace with access to all channels—no idea how it got there.
I got a phishing email that looked like it was from our CFO asking for a “confidential wire transfer”—the domain was off by one letter.
My laptop started making weird noises and the fan is running nonstop—Task Manager shows a process called “miner.exe”.
I just got a notification that my GitHub 2FA was disabled but I didn't do it—someone's trying to hijack my account.

I got a call from someone claiming to be from IT and they asked me to install AnyDesk—sounded fishy so I hung up.
Noticed an unfamiliar AWS IAM role with full admin permissions in our prod-payments account—no one on the team created it.
